mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL > 255) signals central bank inaction, leading to higher real interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 255 despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and labor demand (PAYEMS) indicates strong economic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 for any 3-year window when both INDPRO and PAYEMS show positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in unemployment (UNRATE < 3.5%) during a monetary tightening cycle signals labor market overheating, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where both UNRATE < 3.5% and FEDFUNDS > 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts (HOUST > 1250) after a 3-month decline signals improved consumer confidence, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where HOUST rebounds after a 3-month decline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO < 100) indicates supply-side shocks, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines by more than 5% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 for any 3-year window when both GDPC1 and RSAFS show positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS > 150k) signals strong labor market conditions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where PAYEMS increases by more than 150k.
